product: KIVA (original/legacy, discontinued) — successor is KIVA II

electroacoustic.directivity: 100 degrees symmetric horizontal (above 500 Hz); vertical coverage set by element count and array curvature
electroacoustic.components: LF: 2 x 6.5 in weather-resistant cone drivers, bass-reflex loaded; HF: 1 x 1.5 in diaphragm compression driver on a DOSC waveguide
electroacoustic.max_spl_note: 130 dB peak at 1 m, free field, 10 dB crest-factor pink noise with [KIVA] preset (per original Kiva manual). Note: original Kiva manual specifies 8 ohm nominal impedance; the 16 ohm value reflects the application configuration.

operating_modes: passive 2-way, single amplifier channel per enclosure
amplification.preset_note: [KIVA] is the full-range medium/long-throw line-source preset; [KIVA_FI] is the front-fill/short-throw variant; [KIVA_SB15] is a hybrid preset combining Kiva with the SB15m subwoofer. SB15_100 / SB18_60 (and cardioid _C variants) are the companion subwoofer presets.

connection.input_connector: 2x 4-point speakON (NL4), wired in parallel and interchangeable as IN or LINK; pinout uses point 1 (1+/1-) for the passive enclosure, points 2 not linked
connection.breakouts: SP-Y1 (4-point speakON to 2x 2-point speakON, with CC4FP adapter) for two passive enclosures; DOSUB-LA8 (8-point PA-COM to 4x 2-point speakON) for four enclosures off an LA8/PA-COM output

physical.cabinet: Composite sandwich structure with ZAMAC back plate
physical.front: Plastic grill with Airnet acoustically neutral fabric
physical.finish: Grey brown RAL 8019 or Pure white RAL 9010, custom RAL on special order
physical.protection_rating_note: No IP rating stated for the original Kiva enclosure (weather-resistant transducers); the companion SB15m is rated IP45

modes: flown line-source array (KIBU II or KIBU-SB frame)
pole-mounted (KIET II on 35 mm pole, up to 3 enclosures)
deployment.max_per_flown_array: 20 maximum (12 safe limit) flown on KIBU II or KIBU-SB; pullback limited to 9; stacked limited to 9 (4 safe)
rigging_accessories: KIBU II (flying/stacking frame for Kiva II/Kiva)
KIBU-SB (frame for flying or stacking Kiva II/Kiva with SB15m)
KIET II (ceiling or pole-mount plate, 3 enclosures max)
Captive 3-point rigging system integrated in the enclosure
deployment.deployment_note: Flown/stacked array limits are taken from the shared KIVA II rigging manual, which explicitly covers both KIVA II and the original KIVA on the KIBU II/KIBU-SB/KIET II hardware.

usage: Ultra-compact passive line-source element for vocal-clarity applications where sightlines and visual footprint matter: theatres, houses of worship, corporate and orchestral events, plus fill/delay duties on larger systems.
Each enclosure is passive and 2-way, so it consumes a single amplifier channel; an LA4 drives up to 8 Kiva per controller, maximizing enclosure density per rack.
Use [KIVA] for medium-to-long-throw main hangs and [KIVA_FI] when deploying short-throw front-fill or distributed elements that need independent per-channel control.

Pair with the SB15m subwoofer (via [KIVA_SB15] hybrid preset, sub on OUT1) to extend low-end down toward 40 Hz, or with SB18 (60 Hz) for fuller program material; on its own the enclosure rolls off around 80 Hz.
Fly up to 20 elements (12 safe) on KIBU II/KIBU-SB with inter-element angles up to 15 degrees for tight audience-geometry shaping; ceiling/pole installs via KIET II cap out at 3 enclosures.

Legacy archive product superseded by KIVA II (which moves to 16 ohm, 1.75 in HF, higher 138 dB SPL); plan Kiva deployments around LA4/LA8-era controllers and 8 ohm-native presets even though the app models it at 16 ohm.
